values.title: values
values.description: Repeated field of dynamically typed values.

google.protobuf.ListValue.title: ListValue
google.protobuf.ListValue.description:
`ListValue` is a wrapper around a repeated field of values.

 The JSON representation for `ListValue` is JSON array.

google.protobuf.NullValue.title: NullValue
google.protobuf.NullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
 `Value` type union.

 The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

google.protobuf.Struct.description:
`Struct` represents a structured data value, consisting of fields
 which map to dynamically typed values. In some languages, `Struct`
 might be supported by a native representation. For example, in
 scripting languages like JS a struct is represented as an
 object. The details of that representation are described together
 with the proto support for the language.

 The JSON representation for `Struct` is JSON object.

google.protobuf.Value.description:
`Value` represents a dynamically typed value which can be either
 null, a number, a string, a boolean, a recursive struct value, or a
 list of values. A producer of value is expected to set one of these
 variants. Absence of any variant indicates an error.

 The JSON representation for `Value` is JSON value.

slug.title: slug
slug.description:
The slug is a stable, machine-readable, two-part identifier (e.g., "user.already_exists").
 It is intended for programmatic use and does not change across versions, making it suitable for clients to use in their error handling logic.
 It is built from a namespace / resource (e.g., "user") and a specific error condition (e.g., "already_exists"), separated by a dot.

message.title: message
message.description:
The message is a human-readable message in English.
 This message is intended for developers and should provide a clear explanation of the error.
 It is not meant for end-users and should not be localized.
 Clients are encouraged not to present this to their end-users, but rather use their own translation logic based on the slug.

details.title: details
details.description:
The details field is a structured object that can contain additional information about the error.
 It is intended to provide more context for developers and can include any relevant data that may help in diagnosing the issue
 or clients can use it to provide additional context in their error handling logic.
 The content of the details field is not standardized and can vary depending on the error type.
 While object details should remain consistent across versions, there's no guarantee that the structure will remain unchanged,
 so clients should not rely on a specific structure within the details field.

zitadel.error.v2.ErrorDetail.title: ErrorDetail
zitadel.error.v2.ErrorDetail.description: ErrorDetail is the ZITADEL standard for rich error responses.
